chunks.text: Heart disease refers to a range of conditions affecting the heart, most commonly coronary artery disease, which involves the narrowing of vessels that supply blood to the heart muscle.

Cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains the leading cause of death globally, claiming nearly 20 million lives annually. Prevalence is increasing in low-income regions due to the rapid adoption of sedentary lifestyles.
Symptoms vary but often include chest pain (angina), shortness of breath, palpitations, and fatigue. Some individuals remain asymptomatic until a myocardial infarction (heart attack) occurs.
Primary drivers include atherosclerosis, high blood pressure, elevated LDL cholesterol, and smoking. Emerging 2026 data also highlights the role of chronic inflammation and sleep deprivation.
Diagnosis involves Electrocardiograms (ECG), stress testing, echocardiography, and cardiac catheterization or CT scans to visualize artery blockages.
First-line treatments involve statins for cholesterol and ACE inhibitors for blood pressure. Advanced cases may require percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) or bypass surgery.
Prevention centers on 'Life's Essential 8'—managing blood pressure, cholesterol, and blood sugar while prioritizing physical activity, healthy sleep, and smoking cessation.
